title: Retail pos management of cannabinoid containing products
abstract: Systems and methods are provided for validating that cannabinoid products correspond to specified cannabinoid potencies (e.g. concentration of cannabinoids in the product or total amount - mass/weight of cannabinoids in a product). Product identifying information may be received regarding a product. Such product identifying information and cannabinoid potency information may be stored at an inventory control system, which receives data from a point of sale (POS) system. The data received from the POS system may be compared to data stored at the inventory control system. When information received from a POS system match information stored at the inventory control system, a customer may be authorized to receive tire cannabinoid containing product.
claims: Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for managing cannabinoid containing products, the method comprising: storing received product information in a database, the product information including a plurality of product identifiers each associated with a cannabinoid containing product having respective cannabinoid potency data; receiving a purchase request from a point of sale (P05) system, the purchase request including a product identifier and product validation data; matching the requested product identifier to one of the stored product identifiers, wherein the matching product identifier is associated with cannabinoid potency data; validating the purchase request based on a comparison of the validation data and the associated cannabinoid potency data; and transmitting validation of the purchase request to the POS system. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the cannabinoid potency data includes a set of images, and the product validation data includes at least one image, and wherein validating the purchase request is based on matching the at least one image to the set of images. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising updating an inventory database based on an amount of the purchased cannabinoid containing product 4. The method of clam 1, wherein the cannabinoid potency data includes a first set of test data, and the product validation data includes a second set of test data, wherein validating the purchase request is based on matching the first set to the second set 5. The method of claim 4, further comprising obtaining the first set of testing data using one or more sensors of a testing system. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein the second set of test data is obtained by one or more sensors of the POS system. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the purchase request further includes customer authentication information, and wherein validating the purchase request further includes validating the authentication information. 8. A system for managing cannabinoid containing products, the system comprising: a database that stores received product information, the product information including a plurality of product identifiers each associated with a cannabinoid containing product having respective cannabinoid potency data; a communication interface that receives a purchase request from a point of sale (POS) system, the purchase request including a product identifier and product validation data; and a processor that executes instructions stored in memory, wherein executing of the instructions by the processor: matches the requested product identifier to one of the stored product identifiers, wherein the matching product identifier is associated with cannabinoid potency data; validates the purchase request based on a comparison of the validation data and the associated cannabinoid potency data; wherein the communication interface transmits validation of the purchase request to the POS system. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the cannabinoid potency data includes a set of images, and the product validation data includes at least one image, and wherein the processor validates the purchase request based on matching the at least one image to the set of images. 10. The system of claim 8, further comprising an inventory database that is updated based on an amount of the purchased cannabinoid containing product 11. The system of clam 8, wherein the cannabinoid potency data includes a first set of test data, and the product validation data includes a second set of test data, wherein the processor validates the purchase request based on matching the first set to the second set 12. The system of claim 11, further comprising a testing system that includes one or more sensors configured to obtain the first set of testing data. 13. The system of claim 11, wherein the second set of test data is obtained by one or more sensors of the POS system. 14. The system of claim 9, wherein the purchase request further includes customer authentication information, and wherein the processor validates the purchase request by further validating the authentication information. 15. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium having embodied thereon a program executable by a processor for implementing a method for managing cannabinoid containing products, the method comprising: storing received product information in a database, the product information including a plurality of product identifiers each associated with a cannabinoid containing product having respective cannabinoid potency data; receiving a purchase request from a point of sale (POS) system, the purchase request including a product identifier and product validation data; matching the requested product identifier to one of the stored product identifiers, wherein the matching product identifier is associated with cannabinoid potency data; validating the purchase request based on a comparison of the validation data and the associated cannabinoid potency data; and transmitting validation of the purchase request to the POS system.
